text: Quinazoline derivatives represent a vital class of nitrogen-containing heterocyclic compounds that have garnered significant attention in medicinal chemistry due to their extensive range of therapeutic applications. This review provides a comprehensive overview of recent advancements in the synthetic methodologies and biological evaluations of quinazoline-based molecules. We categorize the diverse synthetic strategies into five primary frameworks: Aza-reactions, microwave-assisted synthesis, metal-mediated transformations, ultrasound-promoted techniques, and phase-transfer catalysis. Furthermore, supplementary methods such as oxidative cyclization, one-pot protocols, and reagent refluxing are examined. In tandem with the chemical synthesis, this paper details the broad spectrum of pharmacological profiles exhibited by these derivatives, including their roles as anti-cancer, anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and anti-obesity agents, among others. By consolidating current knowledge on structure-activity relationships and innovative preparation routes, this review aims to offer a foundational reference for the future design and development of potent quinazoline-based pharmaceuticals and agrochemicals.
